assumptions: The developer has a supported browser, an email address, and a local web stack able to host a server and client page.
No Checkout.com sandbox account, API keys, or Flow integration exists yet.
Sandbox test cards are acceptable for first success.

developer_goal: Create a Checkout.com sandbox account and complete the earliest official first successful test payment path.

first_success.outcome: A sandbox Flow payment completes after Payment Session creation and client mount, subject to unresolved signup-field evidence.
first_success.observable_signal: Get started documents a successful Flow payment and Dashboard Payments status after using the documented test card.

route_selection.surface: Checkout.com Get started Flow guide plus sandbox test-account signup
route_selection.rule: Prefer the documented Get started Flow sandbox path once account-creation fields are known; do not invent alternate Unified API terminals without an official shortest-path winner.

candidates.name: Get started Flow embedded checkout in sandbox
candidates.reason: Official Get started centers Flow Payment Sessions and a test-card Pay step, but sandbox signup fields remain opaque and alternate API UIs are also marketed from the test-account page.

candidates.name: Other Unified API or Frames-style integrations
candidates.reason: Create a test account copy references the Unified API without establishing whether any non-Flow route is shorter to first payment success.

required_path.action: Create a Checkout.com sandbox test account from the official test-account signup entry.

required_fields.label: Sandbox signup fields
required_fields.notes: Support and marketing pages confirm signup exists, but public fetches did not expose the current required form fields, consents, or email verification steps.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can open the sandbox Dashboard and create API keys.

required_path.action: Create a public key and a secret key with the scopes required by Flow.

required_fields.label: Public key scopes payment-sessions:pay and vault-tokenization

required_path.observable_result: Sandbox public and secret keys are available for Payment Sessions.

required_path.action: Configure a webhook endpoint for payment events and retrieve the account sandbox API prefix.
required_path.observable_result: Webhook delivery and the account-specific sandbox base URL are available.

required_path.action: Create a Payment Session server-side, load Checkout Web Components, mount Flow, and complete a test-card payment.
required_path.observable_result: Flow completes a sandbox payment and Dashboard Payments can show the result.

prerequisites.description: A Checkout.com test account is required before creating Flow keys.
A reachable webhook receiver is required to receive payment-status events.

external_gates.description: Additional payment methods beyond cards may require contacting a solutions engineer.

unavoidable_waits.description: Webhook delivery of payment status may arrive after customer redirect.

platform_outcomes.description: Checkout.com returns a Payment Session token used to mount Flow.
Flow performs the payment request and related 3DS or redirect handling after Pay.

excluded.item: Reading customization, localization, and 3DS deep-dive docs
excluded.reason: Get started treats these as next steps after the first test payment.
Get started labels the project template download optional.

sources.title: Create a sandbox account
evidence_supported: APM testing may need solutions engineer

sources.title: Create a test account
evidence_supported: Public test-account marketing and sign-in recovery states
No complete field inventory in fetched HTML

uncertainties.question: What exact fields, consents, verification actions, and email transitions create a Checkout.com sandbox account today?
uncertainties.impact: Required field counts and account-step actions cannot be verified, so the audit cannot become verified even if Flow implementation steps are documented.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated screenshot or read-only walkthrough of https://www.checkout.com/get-test-account through first Dashboard access.

uncertainties.question: Is Flow the sole official shortest first-payment route, or do other Unified API surfaces tie under the lexicographic rule?
uncertainties.impact: A consequential alternate integration could change implementation actions before first payment success.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official comparison or recommendation that names one canonical first sandbox payment route from account creation.
